reasons: Cecilion and Carmilla have a direct Moonlit Waltz interaction that gives Carmilla an entry path from Cecilion's position.
Cecilion can punish enemies affected by Carmilla's linked teamfight setup from safe range.

proof.summary: Carmilla can enter through Cecilion's special interaction, then create a linked control window that Cecilion can punish from range.
worksBestWhen: Cecilion is positioned safely before Carmilla commits.
Carmilla links enemies while Cecilion still has damage skills available.
failureCases: Cecilion is too far to contribute after Carmilla enters.
Carmilla uses her engage before Cecilion is ready.
The enemy spreads out before Curse of Blood connects.

reasons: Carmilla's linked teamfight setup helps keep enemies close enough for Luo Yi's Yin-Yang Reaction value.
Luo Yi can punish clustered enemies with repeated mark reactions after Carmilla starts the fight.

proof.summary: Carmilla creates the close-range teamfight link, while Luo Yi adds AoE magic punishment and displacement pressure through Yin-Yang Reaction.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla starts the fight after Luo Yi is already in casting range.
Luo Yi saves charges for the linked enemy group.
failureCases: Luo Yi has no skill charges when Carmilla engages.
The enemy disengages before Luo Yi can apply opposite marks.

reasons: Carmilla's control and link setup can hold enemies in the area where Odette wants to channel damage.
Odette gives Carmilla's engage a high-value AoE follow-up during the same teamfight window.

proof.summary: Carmilla starts the grouped-control window, then Odette follows with AoE channel damage while enemies are linked or controlled.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla engages before Odette commits to the channel.
Odette casts from a position that cannot be instantly interrupted.
failureCases: Odette is interrupted before damage ramps up.
Carmilla engages too far away from Odette.
The enemy saves hard CC for Odette's channel.

reasons: Carmilla can start fights from the frontline while Pharsa follows from safer range.
Pharsa's long-range burst can punish enemies affected by Carmilla's link and control window.

proof.summary: Carmilla creates the close-range setup, while Pharsa follows from distance with long-range magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla engages inside Pharsa's ultimate range.
Pharsa waits for Carmilla's real commitment before using her burst window.
failureCases: Pharsa is forced to reposition before follow-up.
The enemy dives Pharsa while Carmilla is already committed.

reasons: Carmilla's linked enemy group gives Vexana a clearer window to land AoE control and summon pressure.
Vexana can extend the fight after Carmilla enters by adding zone control around linked targets.

proof.summary: Carmilla starts the teamfight with link and control, then Vexana adds follow-up CC and zone pressure to keep enemies inside the fight.
worksBestWhen: Vexana saves her control skill for Carmilla's engage.
failureCases: Carmilla enters without nearby damage support.
The enemy disengages before Vexana's pressure becomes meaningful.
